title_en: Zakat, Fasting, and Hajj in Al-Muqni'
content_en:
The chapters on financial and ritual worship in Al-Muqni' bring the same intermediate-level thoroughness to zakat, sawm, and hajj that characterizes the work throughout. Ibn Qudamah presents the Hanbali positions with enough detail to be actionable and enough reasoning to be comprehensible, while reserving the full evidential apparatus for Al-Mughni.

The zakat chapter addresses all the standard categories with their nisabs, rates, and conditions, then proceeds to more complex questions that primers cannot handle. On zakat on trade goods, Al-Muqni' discusses the founding evidential basis, the conditions for trade goods to become zakatable (acquisition with the intent to trade, valuation at the end of the hawl), and the rate. Ibn Qudamah addresses the important question of trade goods that have been in a merchant's inventory for multiple years without sale: the hawl is continuous, and zakat is due each year regardless of whether the goods have been sold.

On the distribution of zakat, Al-Muqni' covers the eight categories of recipients from verse 9:60 with their conditions. The question of whether zakat must be distributed to all eight categories simultaneously or may be given entirely to one category is addressed: the Hanbali position is that the giver may concentrate zakat on the neediest category if they are present, without dividing it among all eight. This is a practically important ruling that affects how zakat is distributed in a given community.

The fasting chapter covers the special questions of Ramadan that arise in practice: the ruling on seeing the moon in one region and how it affects other regions, the conditions under which an individual may rely on their own sighting rather than the official announcement, and the question of Ramadan falling in different seasons relative to the length of the day. Ibn Qudamah addresses these questions through the lens of the established Hanbali positions and the hadith evidence.

On hajj, Al-Muqni' is particularly careful on the rites and their sequences. The chapter on the mandatory rites (wajibat) of hajj — those whose omission requires a compensatory blood sacrifice but does not invalidate the hajj — and the pillars (arkan) whose omission cannot be compensated is one of the most practically useful sections for students preparing for or guiding others through the pilgrimage.

The chapter on 'umrah in Al-Muqni' covers its obligation (Hanbali school: it is wajib once in a lifetime), its pillars (ihram, tawaf, and sa'y), its conditions, and the relationship between 'umrah and hajj in the different forms of pilgrimage. Students completing the hajj chapter have a complete picture of the obligations of the fifth pillar.
title_id: Zakat, Puasa, dan Haji dalam Al-Muqni'
content_id:
Bab-bab tentang ibadah finansial dan ritual dalam al-Muqni' membawa ketelitian tingkat menengah yang sama untuk zakat, shaum, dan haji yang menjadi ciri khas karya ini secara keseluruhan. Ibnu Qudamah menyajikan posisi-posisi Hanbali dengan detail yang cukup untuk dapat diterapkan secara praktis, alasan yang cukup untuk membuat posisi-posisi tersebut dapat dipahami, dan perbandingan yang cukup untuk menunjukkan apa yang khas tentang pendekatan Hanbali.

Dalam bab zakat, Ibnu Qudamah mencakup semua kategori harta yang dikenai zakat dalam mazhab Hanbali, termasuk beberapa hal yang khas untuk mazhab ini. Zakat madu dan zakat barang tambang adalah dua area di mana Hanbali memiliki posisi yang lebih komprehensif dari beberapa mazhab lain. Ibnu Qudamah juga membahas pertanyaan tentang apakah zakat boleh dibayarkan sebelum haul (tahun zakat) tiba, dengan menyajikan perdebatan internal mazhab tentang masalah ini.

Dalam bab puasa, pembahasan mencakup penentuan awal Ramadan, fardhu-fardhu puasa, hal-hal yang membatalkan puasa dan konsekuensinya, kondisi yang membolehkan berbuka, kafarat, serta puasa sunnah. Mazhab Hanbali dikenal karena kehati-hatiannya dalam mendefinisikan hal-hal yang membatalkan puasa, dan al-Muqni' mencerminkan kehati-hatian ini dengan mendiskusikan kasus-kasus batas secara rinci.

Dalam bab haji, Ibnu Qudamah menyajikan manasik haji secara lengkap: syarat-syarat wajib haji, rukun-rukun, wajibat, sunnah-sunnah, dan dam yang harus dibayar akibat berbagai pelanggaran. Beliau juga mencakup haji dan umrah dalam berbagai kombinasi (ifrad, qiran, dan tamattu'), menyajikan posisi Hanbali tentang mana yang lebih utama beserta alasannya. Seluruh pembahasan ini menjadikan al-Muqni' panduan yang sangat berguna bagi mereka yang bersiap menunaikan ibadah haji.
